title: Delirium follow-up after discharge

examination.text: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.
examination.rationale: The exact delirium section supports interval changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or informant without asserting persistent delirium.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation

assessment.text: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-completed functional or cognitive assessment and caregiver information without auto-diagnosis.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment

treatment_and_management.text: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact support section supports documenting emerging needs, coordination, clinician-entered plans, follow-up and questions.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

source.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans

medication.text: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.
medication.rationale: The exact transfer section supports reviewing discharge information, medicines and reconciliation after hospital discharge without changing a prescription.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.3.7 — transfer information and medicines reconciliation

source_limitations: Discharge diagnosis, cause, baseline cognition, current medical stability, recurrence, capacity, investigation, medication changes, and escalation remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
